name: Work Jurisdiction Routing
steps: Load official tasks from /input/tasks.json as records containing only task_id and prompt.
Adapt official tasks into internal task records without benchmark answers or evaluator data.
Classify task category internally with deterministic rules and fallback policy.
Assign a Work Jurisdiction for the task scope.
Select an answer schema and compact prompt template.
Pack available evidence within the token budget.
Select an allowed model from ALLOWED_MODELS for Fireworks execution.
For Version 5, check the local certification table before Fireworks execution.
For Version 5, run llama.cpp only when the jurisdiction is LOCAL_CERTIFIED.
For Version 5, fall back through FIREWORKS_BASE_URL when local execution is not certified, fails validation, or times out.
Call the configured provider through the approved base URL.
Write only task_id and answer records to /output/results.json.
Write provider, model, token, validation, repair, fallback, and latency telemetry to separate audit artifacts.
qualification_steps: Load benchmarks/categories/version5_local_category_benchmarks_v2.json with the dedicated benchmark loader.
Validate suite schema, category coverage, unique IDs, unique prompts, progressive difficulty, and evaluator metadata.
Project each benchmark record into an official-format model-visible task containing only task_id and prompt.
Keep category, difficulty, expected answers, accepted answers, rubrics, and evaluator metadata in evaluator-only data.
Run an explicit provider/model candidate through the production execution path.
Evaluate outputs with implemented deterministic evaluators or mark unsafe code evaluators blocked.
Review qualification evidence before updating any runtime authorization registry.

benchmark_boundary.authorization_mutation: never during benchmark execution or live task execution
